Usage: git-wrangler <subcommand> [options]

Available subcommands:

  History Rewriting
    remove-secrets          Permanently purges sensitive files from the entire Git history
    rewrite-authors         Rewrites author and committer names and emails across repositories
    rewrite-commits         Rewrites commit messages to adhere to the Conventional Commits standard
    rewrite-commits-ai      Rewrites commit messages with an OpenAI-compatible AI endpoint
    rewrite-dates           Redistributes commit timestamps to mimic natural human activity

  Local Operations
    commit                  Stages all changes and creates a commit across multiple repositories
    fix-gitignore           Audits and fixes .gitignore files by adding missing entries
    license                 Adds or replaces a LICENSE file across repositories
    rename-branch           Renames a specified branch to a new name across repositories
    reset                   Resets the current branch to exactly match its remote counterpart
    review                  Reviews committed changes before pushing across repositories
    untrack                 Removes tracked files that match .gitignore exclusion rules

  Remote Operations
    clone                   Clones multiple GitHub repositories for a given user
    pull                    Pulls the latest changes for all tracked repositories
    push                    Pushes local commits to remote for all tracked repositories
    rename-repo             Bulk renames GitHub repositories and optionally updates their descriptions.

  Utility
    doctor                  Checks dependencies, install guidance, and update status
    help                    Displays help information for git-wrangler and its subcommands
    info                    Displays detailed information about tracked repositories
    status                  Shows dirty/clean and ahead/behind status of tracked repositories
    uninstall               Uninstalls Git Wrangler from the system
    update                  Updates Git Wrangler to the latest version

Run 'git-wrangler help <subcommand>' for detailed information about a specific command.
